nation: Conseil de la Nation Innu Matimekush – Lac John
one_time_description: Corrections aux exercices antérieurs liées à des rapports d'étapes en retard, bailleurs refusant des dépenses; redressements touchant surplus accumulé, revenus reportés, comptes à recevoir et comptes à payer
economic_notes: GBEs accounted for by modified equity method include: Transport Ferroviaire Tshiuetin inc. (33.33%), Tshiuetin S.E.C., Distribution Pétrolière Naskinnu S.E.C., Pétrole Naskinnuk S.E.C. (31%), Hôtel Innu S.E.C. (100%), Béton Naskinnu S.E.C. (100%), Dépanneur Innu inc. (100%). Council has guaranteed loans for Pétroles Naskinnuk S.E.C. and Hôtel Innu S.E.C. totalling several million dollars.
notes: Audit is qualified due to inability to audit completeness of gasoline sales revenues. Prior year (2019) was also qualified for same reason by predecessor auditor. Large prior period adjustments of $2,661,646 in 2020 and $2,009,986 in 2019 due to late progress reports causing funders to refuse expenses. Seven GBEs accounted for by modified equity method rather than consolidation. Financial statements are in French and cover fiscal year ending March 31, 2020. ISC funding calculated as SAC fixed/flexible contributions ($19,321,047 + $1,461,360 = $20,782,407). Total government transfers include SAC, Health Canada, Institut Tshakapesh, SAA, CDRHPNQ, and Fonds d'habitation. Net financial assets is negative (net debt position) of -$5,592,228. Deferred revenue of $8,571,989 is classified as current liability on balance sheet.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
